user_query:
I'm the controller at Ridgefield Labs. Please process our AR data and produce a complete collections package from the files under input/.

Inputs to read:
- input/invoices.csv — AR invoices with columns: invoice_id, customer, invoice_date (YYYY-MM-DD), due_date (YYYY-MM-DD), amount, amount_due, status
- input/credit_sales.csv — Credit sales for the last 90 days ending on the as-of date, columns: date (YYYY-MM-DD), amount
- input/as_of.json — {"as_of_date": "YYYY-MM-DD"}; use this to calculate days past due
- input/promises.json — Payment promise log with objects: {customer, invoice_id, amount, promise_date (YYYY-MM-DD), status: broken|kept|open}
- input/reserve_config.json — Aging-bucket reserve rates as decimals (e.g., 0.06 = 6%) with keys: current, d1_30, d31_60, d61_90, d91_120, d120_plus
- input/beginning_ar.json — {"beginning_ar": number, "current_ending_ar": number} for CEI

What to produce (write all files under output/):
1) AR aging report (CSV) at output/reports/ar_aging_report.csv
   - Group by customer into buckets: Current, 1-30, 31-60, 61-90, 91-120, 120+
   - Calculate using days past due = (as_of_date − due_date); Current if <= 0 days
   - Include columns: Customer, Current, 1-30, 31-60, 61-90, 91-120, 120+, Total
   - Add a final "Total" row summing all customers

2) Collection priority list (CSV) at output/reports/priority_list.csv
   - Score each customer 1–10 using: Balance weight 40%, Age weight 40%, History weight 20%
     • Balance weight: larger total AR => higher score (normalize across customers)
     • Age weight: higher % in 60+ and 120+ => higher score
     • History weight: more broken promises in input/promises.json => higher score
   - Include columns: Customer, Balance, BalanceScore, AgeScore, HistoryScore, PriorityScore, Rank
   - Rank in descending PriorityScore (tie-breakers: higher 120+ balance, then alphabetically)
   - Clearly flag high-risk accounts (balance > $5k and 60+ days past due, or any invoice 120+ days)

3) Draft collection emails (JSON) at output/drafts/email_drafts.json
   - Create one draft per overdue invoice (exclude Current bucket)
   - Use a five-tier escalation ladder by bucket:
     • 1–30 days: Tier 1 (friendly reminder)
     • 31–60 days: Tier 2 (second notice)
     • 61–90 days: Tier 3 (firm request + payment plan option)
     • 91–120 days: Tier 4 (final notice with escalation warning)
     • 120+ days: Tier 5 (collections/legal escalation notice)
   - Each draft must include: customer, invoice_id, tier (1–5), subject, body, amount, due_date, days_past_due, and a {{PAYMENT_LINK}} placeholder
   - Prepare drafts only — do not send any emails

4) KPI dashboard (Markdown) at output/reports/kpi_dashboard.md
   - Compute DSO for the last 90 days: DSO = (Ending AR Balance / Total Credit Sales) × 90, where Ending AR Balance = sum of amount_due for all open invoices as of as_of_date; Total Credit Sales from input/credit_sales.csv
   - Compute CEI using: CEI = [(Beginning AR + Credit Sales − Ending AR) / (Beginning AR + Credit Sales − Current Ending AR)] × 100, where Beginning AR and Current Ending AR come from input/beginning_ar.json
   - Include: DSO, Best Possible DSO (BPDSO = (Current AR / Total Credit Sales) × 90), CEI, % AR by bucket, and 3–5 concise insights

5) Bad debt reserve schedule (CSV) at output/reports/bad_debt_schedule.csv and journal entry draft (Markdown) at output/reports/bad_debt_journal.md
   - Apply reserve_config.json decimal rates to each bucket balance
   - CSV columns: Bucket, Balance, ReservePercent, ReserveAmount; include a Total row
   - Journal entry draft should show DR Bad Debt Expense, CR Allowance for Doubtful Accounts for the total reserve amount

6) Payment promise follow-up schedule (CSV) at output/reports/follow_up_schedule.csv
   - For each record in input/promises.json, create a follow-up entry with columns: customer, invoice_id, amount, promise_date, follow_up_date (3 days before promise_date), status, notes
   - For status = broken, set notes to indicate escalation is recommended; for status = open, notes should indicate a pre-due reminder; for status = kept, note as closed and no follow-up needed

Implementation notes:
- Use only the data in input/; do not mix with any other client
- Be precise in bucket calculations based on input/as_of.json
- Use reserve_config.json rates as decimals (e.g., 0.2 means 20%)
- Create any needed subdirectories under output/
- Do not include any legal advice or send external communications

Please proceed and write the files exactly to the specified paths.
